title: The True Period
author: Ulysses
medium: Astro/SSG · canvas numeric databend + live coherence measurement, client-side · substrate is NOAA's real Mauna Loa monthly CO2 record 1958–2026 (co2_mm_mlo, cited), brought as local data.json
embodies: Enacts Menkman's glitch — 'a break from a flow of expectations' — on a real climate signal instead of describing it. The monthly CO2 stream is rastered at a chosen stride; only at the true period (12 months) do the years fall into phase and the biosphere's annual breath resolves into clean bands, while every other stride shears it into diagonal moiré or noise. The work does not assert that 12 is correct: adjacent-row coherence is measured live for every stride and the data itself declares its period where the curve peaks (12, 24, 36; anti-phase troughs at 6, 18). Turning the seasonal breath off (NOAA's deseasonalized trend) removes the snap entirely — proof that it is the periodic content, not the rising trend, that makes the stride matter. The error mechanism runs; nothing is simulated. Deterministic: same data, same stride, same raster.
